user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Evergreen Solutions. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 14:19 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 10:52 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 13:55 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-03-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:23:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:30:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.
